key_insight: Adding SageMath as a verifiable tool inside a ReAct-style LLM agent substantially improves performance on research-level math problems, especially for computational exploration.
engineering_relevance: This suggests CAS-augmented agents can be a practical workflow for mathematicians and mathematical software systems that need checkable intermediate reasoning, documentation lookup, and faster conjecture testing.
method: ReAct-style agent combines LLM reasoning with SageMath for executable feedback.
Context7 supplies up-to-date documentation during problem solving.
RealMath benchmark was refined with multi-step post-processing and multi-stage validation.
evidence_signal: Across frontier models on RealMath, SageMath access improved solve rates by an average of 9.7 percentage points, with gains ranging from 1.5 to 27.8 points; GPT-5.5 reached 75.2% solve rate and lowest token usage among tool-enabled setups.
watch_item: Results depend on benchmark extraction and validation quality, so tool access gains may be sensitive to dataset filtering, problem selection, and SageMath integration reliability.
paper_title: Evaluating SageMath-Augmented LLM Agents for Computational and Experimental Mathematics
